title: Selective access system for a feeder
abstract: A selective access system, and methods of making and using such a selective access system, whereby the selective access system includes a container including an interior space defined by a side wall disposed between opposing top and bottom walls; an opening disposed within the side wall, the opening communicating with the interior space; a cover slidably engaged with the container, the cover configured to slide upward and downward between a first position and a second position; whereby in the first position, the cover overlays the opening; and whereby in the second position, the cover disposes away from at least a portion of the opening to permit access to the interior space; and a force generator coupled to the cover, the force generator generating a pre-selected force amount which when unopposed, provides the first position of the cover.
claims:
Claims IV. CLAIMS 1. A selective access system comprising: a container comprising an interior space defined by a side wall disposed between opposing top and bottom walls; an opening disposed within said side wall, said opening communicating with said interior space; a cover slidably engaged with said container, said cover configured to slide upward and downward between a first position and a second position; wherein in said first position, said cover overlays said opening; and wherein in said second position, said cover disposes away from at least a portion of said opening to permit access to said interior space; a force generator coupled to said cover, said force generator generating a pre-selected force amount which when unopposed, provides said first position of said cover; and a stop assembly coupled to said selective access system, said stop assembly comprising a stop element and an engagement element; wherein engagement of said stop element with said engagement element stops upward sliding travel of said cover; and wherein at least one of said stop element and said engagement element disposes within said interior space. 2. The selective access system of claim 1, wherein both said stop element and said engagement element dispose within said interior space. 3. The selective access system of claim 2, wherein said stop element is coupled to said cover. 4. The selective access system of claim 3, wherein said stop element inwardly extends from said cover into said interior space. 5. The selective access system of claim 4, wherein said stop element comprises a protrusion which outwardly extends from a cover rear face of said cover. 6. The selective access system of claim 4, wherein said engagement element is coupled to said container. 7. The selective access system of claim 6, wherein said engagement element is coupled to at least one of said side wall or said top wall. 8. The selective access system of claim 7, wherein said engagement element is integrated with at least one of said side wall or said top wall. 9. The selective access system of claim 1 , wherein said cover is configured to slide vertically between said first position and said second position. 10. The selective access system of claim 9, wherein said cover slidably engages with said side wall. 1 1. The selective access system of claim 1 , wherein said second position is provided by a counterforce generator which generates a counterforce amount which is greater than said pre- selected force amount. 12. The selective access system of claim 11 , wherein said force generator comprises a weight element which generates said pre-selected force amount. 13. The selective access system of claim 12, wherein said pre-selected force amount is selected to require said counterforce generator to comprise a specific grouping of animals. 14. The selective access system of claim 13, wherein said specific grouping of animals comprises a taxonomic rank. 15. The selective access system of claim 14, wherein said taxonomic rank is selected from the group consisting of: family, genus, and species. 16. The selective access system of claim 15, wherein said family comprises Snidae. 17. The selective access system of claim 15, wherein said genus comprises Sus. 18. The selective access system of claim 15, wherein said species comprises Sus scrofa. 19. The selective access system of claim 13, wherein said specific grouping of animals comprises wild boars or feral hogs. 20. The selective access system of claim 19, wherein said pre-selected force amount is not less than about 6.8 kilograms. 21. The selective access system of claim 19, wherein said pre-selected force amount is in a range of between about 6.8 kilograms to about 15.9 kilograms. 22. The selective access system of claim 1, wherein said weight element is integrated with said cover. 23. The selective access system of claim 22, wherein a cover weight provides said weight element. 24. The selective access system of claim 23, wherein said cover weight is provided by a material which forms said cover. 25. The selective access system of claim 24, wherein said material comprises steel. 26. The selective access system of claim 25, wherein said material comprises at least 14 gauge steel. 27. The selective access system of claim 23, wherein said cover weight is uniformly distributed within a cover periphery which bounds said cover. 28. The selective access system of claim 1, further comprising a cover actuator coupled to said cover to facilitate disposition of said cover in said second position. 29. The selective access system of claim 28, wherein said cover actuator comprises an elongate member which outwardly extends from a cover front face of said cover. 30. The selective access system of claim 1, further comprising a prop and a bore; wherein upon reception within said bore, said prop supports said cover in said second position. 31. The selective access system of claim 1 , further comprising one or more aperture elements disposed within said cover. 32. The selective access system of claim 1, further comprising an access door pivotally coupled to said top wall. 33. The selective access system of claim 32, further comprising a catch and a corresponding catch-receiving element; wherein engagement of said catch and catch-receiving element secures said door in a closed position. 34. The selective access system of claim 1 , further comprising an anchor assembly to facilitate anchoring said selective access system to a support surface. 35. The selective access system of claim 1, further comprising an ingestible matter guide assembly to facilitate guiding said ingestible matter disposed within said interior space toward said opening. 36. The selective access system of claim 1, wherein only one said opening is disposed within said side wall. 37. The selective access system of claim 1, further comprising a plurality of said openings disposed within said side wall. 38. The selective access system of claim 37, further comprising: a first opening disposed within a front side wall component of said side wall; and a second opening disposed within a rear side wall component of said side wall. 39. The selective access system of claim 38, further comprising: a first cover disposed proximate said first opening; and a second cover disposed proximate said second opening. 40. The selective access system of claim 1, wherein said ingestible matter comprises bait including a toxic agent. 41. A method of making a selective access system, comprising: providing a container comprising an interior space defined by a side wall disposed between opposing top and bottom walls; disposing an opening within said side wall, said opening communicating with said interior space; slidably engaging a cover with said container, said cover configured to slide upward and downward between a first position and a second position; wherein in said first position, said cover overlays said opening; and wherein in said second position, said cover disposes away from at least a portion of said opening to permit access to said interior space; coupling a force generator to said cover, said force generator generating a pre-selected force amount which when unopposed, provides said first position of said cover; a stop element and an engagement element; wherein engagement of said stop element with said engagement element stops upward sliding travel of said cover; and 

 disposing at least one of said stop element and said engagement element within said interior space. 42. The method of claim 41, further comprising disposing both said stop element and said engagement element within said interior space. 43. The method of claim 42, further comprising coupling said stop element to said cover. 44. The method of claim 43, further comprising coupling said stop element to said cover such that said stop element inwardly extends from said cover into said interior space. 45. The method of claim 44, further comprising configuring said stop element to comprise a protrusion which outwardly extends from a cover rear face of said cover. 46. The method of claim 44, further comprising coupling said engagement element to said container. 47. The method of claim 46, further comprising coupling said engagement element to at least one of said side wall or said top wall. 48. The method of claim 47, further comprising integrating said engagement element with at least one of said side wall or said top wall. 49. The method of claim 41, further comprising configuring said cover to slide vertically between said first position and said second position. 50. The method of claim 49, further comprising slidably engaging said cover with said side wall. 51. The method of claim 41, wherein said second position is provided by a counterforce generator which generates a counterforce amount which is greater than said pre-selected force amount. 52. The method of claim 51 , further comprising configuring said force generator to comprise a weight element which generates said pre-selected force amount. 53. The method of claim 52, further comprising selecting said pre-selected force amount to require said counterforce generator to comprise a specific grouping of animals. 54. The method of claim 53, wherein said specific grouping of animals comprises a taxonomic rank. 55. The method of claim 54, wherein said taxonomic rank is selected from the group consisting of: family, genus, and species. 56. The method of claim 55, wherein said family comprises Suidae. 57. The method of claim 55, wherein said genus comprises Sus. 58. The method of claim 55, wherein said species comprises Sus scrofa. 59. The method of claim 53, wherein said specific grouping of animals comprises wild boars or feral hogs. 60. The method of claim 59, wherein said pre-selected force amount is not less than about 6.8 kilograms. 61. The method of claim 59, wherein said pre-selected force amount is in a range of between about 6.8 kilograms to about 15.9 kilograms. 62. The method of claim 41, further comprising integrating said weight element with said cover. 63. The method of claim 62, wherein a cover weight provides said weight element. 64. The method of claim 63, wherein said cover weight is provided by a material which forms said cover. 65. The method of claim 64, wherein said material comprises steel. 66. The method of claim 65, wherein said material comprises at least 14 gauge steel. 67. The method of claim 63, wherein said cover weight is uniformly distributed within a cover periphery which bounds said cover. 68. The method of claim 41, further comprising coupling a cover actuator to said cover to facilitate disposition of said cover in said second position. 69. The method of claim 68, further comprising configuring said cover actuator to comprise an elongate member which outwardly extends from a cover front face of said cover. 70. The method of claim 41, further comprising providing a prop and a bore; wherein upon reception within said bore, said prop supports said cover in said second position. 71. The method of claim 41, further comprising disposing one or more aperture elements within said cover. 72. The method of claim 41 , further comprising pivotally coupling an access door to said top wall. 73. The method of claim 72, further comprising providing a catch and a corresponding catchreceiving element: wherein engagement of said catch and catch-receiving element secures said door in a closed position. 74. The method of claim 41, further comprising providing an anchor assembly to facilitate anchoring said selective access system to a support surface. 75. The method of claim 41, further comprising providing an ingestible matter guide assembly to facilitate guiding said ingestible matter disposed within said interior space toward said opening. 76. The method of claim 41, further comprising disposing only one said opening within said side wall. 77. The method of claim 41 , further comprising disposing a plurality of said openings within said side wall. 78. The method of claim 77, further comprising: disposing a first opening within a front side wall component of said side wall; and disposing a second opening within a rear side wall component of said side wall. 79. The method of claim 78, further comprising: slidably engaging a first cover with said container proximate said first opening; and slidably engaging a second cover with said container proximate said second opening. 80. The method of claim 41, wherein said ingestible matter comprises bait including a toxic agent. 81. A method of using a selective access system for permitting selective access to ingestible matter disposed within said selective access system, comprising: obtaining said selective access system comprising: a container comprising an interior space defined by a side wall disposed between opposing top and bottom walls; an opening disposed within said side wall, said opening communicating with said interior space; a cover slidably engaged with said container, said cover configured to slide upward and downward between a first position and a second position; wherein in said first position, said cover overlays said opening; and wherein in said second position, said cover disposes away from at least a portion of said opening to permit access to said interior space; a force generator coupled to said cover, said force generator generating a preselected force amount which when unopposed, provides said first position of said cover; and a stop assembly coupled to said selective access system, said stop assembly comprising a stop element and an engagement element; wherein engagement of said stop element with said engagement element stops upward sliding travel of said cover; and wherein at least one of said stop element and said engagement element disposes within said interior space; disposing said ingestible matter within said interior space; and disposing said cover in said first position. 82. The method of claim 81, wherein said second position is provided by a counterforce generator which generates a counterforce amount which is greater than said pre-selected force amount. 83. The method of claim 82, further comprising providing bait as said ingestible matter. 84. The method of claim 83, further comprising combining a toxic agent with said bait. 85. The method of claim 82, further comprising selecting said pre-selected force amount to require said counterforce generator to comprise a specific grouping of animals. 86. The method of claim 85, further comprising selecting said pre-selected force amount to require said counterforce generator to comprise a taxonomic rank. 87. The method of claim 86, wherein said taxonomic rank is selected from the group consisting of: family, genus, and species. 88. The method of claim 87, further comprising selecting said pre-selected force amount to require said counterforce generator to comprise Suidae. 89. The method of claim 87, further comprising selecting said pre-selected force amount to require said counterforce generator to comprise Sus. 90. The method of claim 87, further comprising selecting said pre-selected force amount to require said counterforce generator to comprise Sus scrofa. 91. The method of claim 85, further comprising selecting said pre-selected force amount to require said counterforce generator to comprise wild boars or feral hogs. 92. The method of claim 91, further comprising selecting said pre-selected force amount to be not less than about 6.8 kilograms.
